tweets.text: OpenAI co-founder Greg Brockman describes Codex workspace agents running on a cloud-hosted harness with tools and tasks scoped per session. Practical architecture signal for teams building production agentic coding systems on top of Codex.

François Chollet argues that evaluating AGI by how well systems mimic human outputs is a category error. True AGI should be measured by how well a system learns to do things it was not explicitly trained on, including tasks humans themselves cannot perform.

François Chollet argues that LLMs fundamentally lack metacognition: they cannot accurately assess what they know or don't know, producing confident errors and unreliable self-evaluation. He frames this as a key gap separating current systems from robust general reasoning, with direct implications for building reliable agentic pipelines.

Yann LeCun pushes back on p(doom) framing, arguing the real AI risk is not autonomous superintelligence but concentration of AI power in a few companies and misuse by state or corporate actors. He calls for open-source AI development and international governance structures as the appropriate countermeasures.
OpenAI Images v2, an upgraded image generation capability, is now available across all ChatGPT tiers. Confirmed via Logan Kilpatrick on X.
Anthropic announces Claude Design and is hiring senior designers at $400K+ compensation. Signals a design-first product direction for Claude alongside the engineering-centric Claude Code push.

Google announced Gemini features in Chrome are expanding to more Asia-Pacific countries, adding page summarization, image editing, and broader integrations. The rollout extends Google's browser-native AI layer to a wider international audience as part of Chrome's AI-first roadmap.

Andrej Karpathy highlights Bryan Johnson's deep-dive into personal health analytics using Claude across 1.5 billion data points. Demonstrates frontier use of LLMs for personal longitudinal data reasoning at extreme scale.
